summary: Opera by Giacomo Puccini, premiered in 1896 and conducted at its premiere by Arturo Toscanini. Set in the Latin Quarter of Paris, it follows a group of bohemian artists — especially the poet Rodolfo and his love Mimi, a seamstress who dies of tuberculosis. Also features the flirtatious Musetta and her lover Marcello. The Metropolitan Opera's most-performed opera. Basis for Jonathan Larson's musical Rent.

works.name: La Bohème
works.description: Verismo opera by Giacomo Puccini, premiered February 1, 1896, conducted by Arturo Toscanini. Set in the Latin Quarter of Paris, based on Henri Murger's novel/play Scènes de la vie de bohème. The Met's most performed opera. Jonathan Larson's musical Rent is based on it. Ruggero Leoncavallo also wrote a competing La Bohème. Wikipedia

works.name: Mimi – The Heroine
works.description: The female lead of La Bohème: a seamstress who falls in love with the poet Rodolfo and dies of tuberculosis (consumption) at the end of the opera. Introduced in the aria 'Mi chiamano Mimì' (Si, mi chiamano Mimì). The corresponding character in Rent is also named Mimi. Wikipedia

clues.clue: A seamstress who dies of tuberculosis (consumption) at the end
examples: the tenor aria 'Che gelida manina' is sung as an excuse for Rodolfo to get closer to Mimi, who later dies of tuberculosis
This seamstress dies of consumption at the end of the opera in which she appears.

clues.clue: Introduces herself in the aria 'Mi chiamano Mimì' (Si, mi chiamano Mimì)
examples: Mimi...sings the aria 'Si, mi chiamano' to introduce herself to Rodolfo.
Another character in this opera sings 'Vecchia zimarra' as he pawns an object...to buy medicine for another character, who introduces herself in an aria that begins 'Mi chiamano.'

clues.clue: Rodolfo hides her key when a candle goes out, leading to 'Che gelida manina' (Your Tiny Hand is Frozen)
examples: When a candle goes out, Rodolfo and the tuberculosis-suffering Mimi sing 'Che gelida manina.'
Rodolfo...had sung 'Che gelida manina' to her after hiding her key.

works.name: "O soave fanciulla" and the Act I Love Duet
works.description: The closing love duet of Act I, sung by Rodolfo and Mimi. Rodolfo praises Mimi ('la biondinetta') after seeing her face bathed in moonlight; he sings a high C in a unison perdendosi ending. Three descending chords open the moonlit scene. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Rodolfo sees Mimi's face bathed in moonlight and sings 'O soave fanciulla'; the tenor sings a unison perdendosi high C to end the duet
examples: This opera's tenor lead realizes that he has fallen in love when he sees the face of his beloved bathed in moonlight, which prompts the duet 'O soave fanciulla.'
A tenor in an opera with this title often sings a unison, perdendosi high C to end a duet whose female soloist is 'bathed in moonlight.'

clues.clue: Three descending chords open this moonlit love scene near the end of Act I
examples: Three descending chords open a moonlit love duet near the end of this opera's first act.

works.name: Musetta and "Quando me'n vo" (Musetta's Waltz)
works.description: Musetta is the flirtatious companion of Marcello. Her signature aria 'Quando me'n vo'' (also called Musetta's Waltz) is sung in 3/4 time at the Café Momus. She pretends her shoe hurts to send away her rich lover Alcindoro, then sings to attract Marcello by boasting that people stare at her beauty when she walks down the street. Wikipedia

clues.clue: 'Quando me'n vo' is a 3/4-time aria in which Musetta boasts 'When I walk alone in the street, people stop and stare at me'; sung to make Marcello jealous at the Café Momus
examples: A character in this opera sings about how men stare at her beauty when she walks down the street in the aria 'Quando me'n vo,' or her namesake waltz, which is intended to make Marcello jealous.
Musetta sings her waltz in this Giacomo Puccini opera. At the end of this opera, the poet Rodolfo bemoans Mimi's death from tuberculosis.

clues.clue: Musetta pretends her shoe hurts to send Alcindoro away; characters leave the café without paying
examples: The singer of this aria pretends that her shoe is paining her to send her rich lover, Alcindoro, away.
Musetta performs 'Quando M'en Vo' while at Cafe Momus with this man, her sugar daddy. The characters eventually run out of the cafe, leaving this man to pay their bill.

works.name: "Vecchia zimarra" – Colline's Coat Aria
works.description: Aria sung by the philosopher Colline as he pawns his beloved overcoat to buy medicine for the dying Mimi. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Colline sings 'Vecchia zimarra' while pawning his coat to buy medicine for Mimi
examples: A character in this opera sings 'Vecchia zimarra' as he pawns an object that was bought with Schaunard's horn and a bonnet to buy medicine for another character.

comprehensive_summary:
La Bohème is a verismo opera by Giacomo Puccini, premiered February 1, 1896, with Arturo Toscanini conducting. It is based on Henri Murger's novel Scènes de la vie de bohème, and is the Metropolitan Opera's most-performed opera. Jonathan Larson's musical Rent is based on it, with the female lead of Rent also named Mimi and 'Musetta's Waltz' specifically referenced in the musical.

The opera is set in the Latin Quarter of Paris and follows a group of impoverished bohemian artists: the poet Rodolfo, the painter Marcello, the philosopher Colline, and the musician Schaunard. In Act I, set in their attic garret, Rodolfo burns his play for warmth and Schaunard arrives having been paid by an Englishman to play violin until his parrot died. The landlord Benoit arrives to collect rent on Christmas Eve. Mimi, a seamstress from downstairs, knocks at the door — her candle has gone out. Rodolfo hides her key while relighting her candle, and sings 'Che gelida manina' (Your Tiny Hand is Frozen). Mimi introduces herself in 'Mi chiamano Mimì.' The act closes with the love duet 'O soave fanciulla,' opened by three descending chords; Rodolfo, seeing Mimi's face bathed in moonlight, sings a unison perdendosi high C at the duet's close.

At the Café Momus in Act II, the group meets Musetta, who arrives with her rich admirer Alcindoro. Musetta sings her waltz 'Quando me'n vo'' in 3/4 time, boasting that people stare at her beauty in the street, intending to make Marcello jealous. She pretends her shoe hurts to send Alcindoro away. When a military procession passes, the group slips out without paying, leaving Alcindoro with the bill.

In Act III, Mimi and Rodolfo part; Mimi receives a pink bonnet and laments the separation in 'Donde lieta uscì.' In Act IV, Mimi returns dying of tuberculosis. Colline sings 'Vecchia zimarra' while pawning his beloved overcoat (originally bought with Schaunard's horn money and a bonnet) to buy medicine. Mimi dies and Rodolfo is overcome with grief.

Puccini and librettist Giuseppe Giacosa (who resigned three times) were accused of stealing the libretto by Ruggero Leoncavallo, who wrote a competing La Bohème rewritten to focus on Marcello and Musetta. Puccini reportedly replied, 'Let him compose, I will compose. The audience will decide.'
